items.title: Coinbase highlights Algorand, Aptos' work to mitigate quantum threat
items.summary: Coinbase’s quantum advisory board says quantum computing isn’t yet a threat, but has urged for upgrade work to begin, with some blockchains being less prepared than others.

items.title: Cut the red tape: 39 financial giants demand an emergency fast-track for Europe's blockchain pilot
items.summary: The firms requested separating the DLT pilot regime from a larger package of 18 financial laws to allow for quicker updates and build real markets.

items.title: Mutation testing for the agentic era
items.summary: Code coverage is one of the most dangerous quality metrics in software testing. Many developers fail to realize that code coverage lies by omission: it measures execution, not verification. Test suites with high coverage can obfuscate the fact that critical functionality is untes

items.title: Try our new dimensional analysis Claude plugin
items.summary: We’re releasing a new Claude plugin for developing and auditing code that implements dimensional analysis, a technique we explored in our most recent blog post. Most LLM-based security skills ask the model to find bugs. Our new dimensional-analysis plugin for Claude Code takes a

items.title: Spotting issues in DeFi with dimensional analysis
items.summary: Using dimensional analysis, you can categorically rule out a whole category of logic and arithmetic bugs that plague DeFi formulas. No code changes required, just better reasoning! One of the first lessons in physics is learning to think in terms of dimensions. Physicists can oft

items.title: European banks are at risk of losing customers to rivals with better crypto tools
items.summary: A new study found that while European investors find crypto complex, they are increasingly willing to switch banks to find a trusted partner that offers secure and regulated digital asset services.

items.title: Fed chair nominee pressed on potential conflicts of interest, independence
items.summary: Kevin Warsh faced intense questions from Senator Elizabeth Warren and other lawmakers over his more than $100 million financial disclosure ahead of a confirmation hearing.

items.title: Group of 39 firms urge EU to fast-track DLT rules, warn of lagging US
items.summary: A 39-member coalition including Nasdaq urged the EU to fast-track DLT pilot changes, warning Europe could lose ground to the US in tokenized finance.

items.title: VIX drops 45% in three weeks: Is Bitcoin price ready to retake $80K?
items.summary: A falling VIX signals improving risk appetite, boosting Bitcoin’s chances of attracting fresh demand and breaking above the $80,000 level.

items.title: Bitcoin's 'Coinbase premium' just posted its longest bullish streak since October's record high of $126,000
items.summary: Bitcoin's Coinbase premium has been positive for 14 straight days, the longest bullish streak since BTC hit its all time high of $126,000 in October.

items.title: Bitcoin climbs to $78,100 on Trump ceasefire extension, Strategy's $2.5 billion buy
items.summary: Bitcoin traded at $77,541 on Wednesday morning, up 2.2% over 24 hours and 4.3% on the week, after Trump extended the Iran ceasefire and Strategy disclosed its largest BTC purchase in 17 months.

items.title: Tron's Justin Sun sues Trump-linked World Liberty Financial over frozen assets
items.summary: World Liberty unfairly froze Sun's $WLFI tokens and threatened him, the lawsuit alleged.

items.title: Chinese crypto mogul Li Lin’s private trading arm is set to move into a Hong Kong-listed wealth firm
items.summary: The transfer effectively moves part of Li’s private trading operation into a public company where he is the largest shareholder.

items.title: AI drives surge in ‘bug bounty’ reports, but the ‘slop’ is rising too
items.summary: HackerOne, one of the largest bug bounty platforms in the world, reported there were 85,000 valid bounty submissions in 2025, up 7% from the previous year.

items.title: Privacy protocol Umbra shuts front end to stifle Kelp exploiters
items.summary: Umbra has shut down its front end to stop hackers, but says it can’t stop the use of its smart contracts or another version of its open-source front end.

items.title: US law firm apologizes after AI hallucinations made it to a legal filing
items.summary: Sullivan & Cromwell’s Andrew Dietderich said the company has AI policies to prevent incorrect citations and other errors, but procedures weren’t followed on this occasion.

items.title: Justin Sun sues World Liberty Financial over token lockup
items.summary: Sun said the suit is to protect his rights as a token holder, but he remains a supporter of President Donald Trump and his administration’s efforts to make the US crypto-friendly.

items.title: Inside the MAS Sandbox: How Ripple is testing RLUSD for real trade settlements
items.summary: How Ripple is testing RLUSD in Singapore’s MAS sandbox to enable conditional trade settlements, and why this does not amount to full regulatory approval.

items.title: US admiral calls Bitcoin an instrument for US ‘power projection’
items.summary: US Navy Admiral Samuel Paparo said Bitcoin’s proof-of-work technology has "really important" computer science applications when it comes to cybersecurity.

items.title: Crypto's great hope in Senate's Clarity Act still has a path to survive tight calendar
items.summary: A sideshow stablecoin yield debate has dragged the market structure bill through months of delay, even as the Senate's available floor time diminishes for 2026.
